description: Bill 15, introduced by the Quebec government under Christian Dubé, aimed to centralize the healthcare system through the creation of 'Santé Québec'. The debate centered on fears of increased privatization versus the need for administrative efficiency in a strained post-pandemic system.

key_events.description: Public health unions launch a major communications campaign against Bill 15.
Government launches a provincial 'Fixing the System' PR campaign.
Legislature passes closure motion to limit debate and force a vote.
Bill 15 is passed into law in late 2023.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling public sentiment when reforms are framed as 'necessary efficiency' against 'creeping privatization'.
